data.narrative:
Raphe mPhibr is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Vivek and Vikash Mishra that has raised $145M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms—all operating in the Defense & Security sector at equivalent or proximate stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses $145M in total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $276M. The peer-median method benchmarks against 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables, which span a range of $300M to $3.0B and cluster around a median of $1.0B. The named peers in this set—Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B)—demonstrate material dispersion, reflecting heterogeneity in growth trajectory and technology differentiation within the defense subsector.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $234M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $638M. The significant spread reflects uncertainty in where Raphe positions relative to its peer set: whether closer to stage-typical progression multiples or to the higher end of sector-comparable valuations. Capital efficiency relative to raised funds and product-market traction in defense procurement cycles represent key discriminators for narrowing this range.
